name: Preserved Lemons
subtitle: North African salt-cured citrus gold
blrNote: Small, thin-skinned Indian nimbus are actually *superior* for this than thick-skinned Western lemons. They cure faster and have less bitter pith.

ingredients.name: Lemons
substitutions: Indian lime (gondhoraj) for a floral twist

ingredients.name: Rock Salt
substitutions: Sea salt, kosher salt - never iodised

ingredients.name: Extra lemon juice
ingredients.notes: To top up if lemons aren't submerged

tldr: Stuff lemons with salt, smash into a jar. Wait until the skin turns into edible velvet.

steps.title: The X-Cut
steps.instruction: Cut lemons into quarters, but only 3/4 of the way down so they stay attached at the base. Think of it as quartering without separating.
tips: A sharp knife is essential - dull knives will crush the lemon and waste juice
Choose lemons with thin, smooth skin - they cure faster and have less bitter pith than thick-skinned varieties.
Scrub the lemons well before cutting - the rind is what you'll be eating, so clean it thoroughly.
steps.checkpoint: Lemons open like flowers but still connected at the base

steps.title: The Salt Stuff
steps.instruction: Pack the inside of each lemon with a generous tablespoon of salt. Open the cuts and rub salt deep inside. Be liberal.
tips: More salt = faster cure. You cannot over-salt these.
Use coarse rock salt rather than fine - it packs into the cuts better and dissolves slowly for a more even cure.
Work over a bowl to catch any juice that escapes during salting - pour it back into the jar later.
steps.checkpoint: Each lemon visibly packed with salt crystals

steps.title: The Smash
steps.instruction: Pack into a sterilized jar. Press down HARD with a wooden spoon to release juice. Juice MUST cover the lemons. Add extra fresh lemon juice if needed.
tips: Really crush them - you want every air pocket eliminated
Keep 2–3 extra lemons on hand for juicing - if your packed lemons aren't fully submerged, squeeze fresh juice over the top immediately.

steps.title: The Long Wait
steps.instruction: Store in a cool, dark spot. Flip the jar upside down every few days for the first week to redistribute the salt. After 30 days, taste the rind - it should be soft and mellow, not bitter.
tips: Month 1: still sharp. Month 2: perfect. Month 3: transcendent.
The jar is shelf-stable once fully cured - no refrigeration needed
Always use a clean, dry spoon to fish out lemons - introducing water or food particles into the jar can cause mould.
steps.checkpoint: Rind is soft enough to mash with a fork

images.caption: Preserved Lemons
images.alt: Preserved Lemons - North African salt-cured citrus gold

culturalContext.story: Essential to Moroccan Tagines. In the Maghreb, these lemons aren’t just a condiment; they are the "soul" of the dish, providing a salty, umami-rich citrus hit that fresh lemons cannot replicate.
culturalContext.historicalNote: Salt preservation of citrus in North Africa predates refrigeration by over a thousand years. Caravans crossing the Sahara carried preserved lemons as a source of vitamin C and flavour.
culturalContext.significance: This is the condiment that separates a home-cooked Moroccan tagine from a restaurant imitation. Once you have a jar of these, your cooking permanently upgrades.
culturalContext.funFact: You only eat the rind! The flesh is usually discarded after cooking because it’s too salty. The rind becomes the star - silky, fragrant, and intensely citrusy without any bitterness.

thingsToAccountFor.title: Exposed rinds
thingsToAccountFor.description: Any part of the lemon sticking out of the juice will grow mould. Add extra fresh lemon juice if they aren't submerged.

thingsToAccountFor.title: White film on the brine
thingsToAccountFor.description: Kahm yeast - harmless but ugly. Skim it off. It does not mean the batch is ruined.

dehydratorIntegration.method: Slice cured rinds thin, dehydrate at 45°C for 6–8 hours.
dehydratorIntegration.result: Crispy lemon salt chips - grind into powder for an incredible finishing salt.
tips: The powder is spectacular on grilled vegetables, pasta, and fish

variations.name: Spiced Preserved Lemons
variations.description: Add bay leaves, peppercorns, and a cinnamon stick to the jar.

variations.name: Chilli Preserved Lemons
variations.description: Add dried red chillies and cumin seeds for a North Indian twist.

variations.name: Quick Preserved Lemons
variations.description: Dice lemons small and use 3x the salt - ready in 7 days but less nuanced.
